1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128

3	## Critic-disclosure 3-tier vocabulary (applies to all lanes)
4	
5	Every closed lemma in your task_result MUST classify the body as one of:
6	
7	1. **kernel-clean (this body)** — `#print axioms <lemma>` returns
8	   `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (the kernel baseline only).
9	2. **kernel-clean modulo upstream `<X>` / `<Y>`** — local body has no
10	   `sorry`, but `#print axioms <lemma>` returns `…, sorryAx, …` because
11	   the transitive dependency `<X>` or `<Y>` is sorry-bodied. List the
12	   named upstream sorry carriers explicitly.
13	3. **kernel-clean (transitively)** — `#print axioms <lemma>` returns
14	   the kernel baseline AND every transitive dependency is sorry-free.

24	## Lane A — `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean`
25	
26	**Target**: Close the body of `Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff`
27	(line ~192 post plan-phase refactor; signature `∃ s, lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField P hP s = f`).
28	
29	**Background**: The plan-phase `refactor ocofp-globalsections-sig`
30	mutated the signature so the iff binds the global section `s` to the
31	rational function `f`. This Lane A fills the body. Both directions are
32	documented inside the existing `globalSections_iff` body (forward =
33	Hartshorne II.7.7(b); backward = II.7.7(a)).
34	
35	**Two sub-cases (both directions)**:
36	
37	- **Forward (II.7.7(b))**: given the order conditions on `f`, construct
38	  `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` with `toFunctionField P hP s = f`. Concretely:
39	  the rational function `f` (with at most a simple pole at `P` and
40	  holomorphic at every other prime divisor) lifts to a Cartier section
41	  of the locally-free sheaf `𝒪_C(P)`; `s` is the global section it
42	  defines (Hartshorne II.7.7(b)'s construction).
43	- **Backward (II.7.7(a))**: given `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` with
44	  `toFunctionField P hP s = f`, read off the order conditions
45	  stalk-by-stalk via the DVR identification
46	  `𝒪_C(P)_Q = 𝒪_{C,Q}` for `Q ≠ P` and
47	  `𝒪_C(P)_P = f_P⁻¹ · 𝒪_{C,P}`.
48	
49	**Blocker**: BOTH directions consume the body of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`
50	(L140), which is still a typed `sorry`. If you cannot fire either
51	direction without that body, your lane is correctly PARTIAL — leave the
52	forward + backward sub-cases as named honest sorries (one per direction,
53	with detailed comments citing the missing infrastructure piece) and
54	move on. Do NOT introduce a placeholder body for `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`
55	itself — that path was already analysed iter-180 (Lane D task_result) as
56	blocked on Sheaf-internal-Hom + ModuleCat-forget Mathlib gaps.

11	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 4): the `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_×` and product-stability instances
12

14	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
15	
16	* the chart-bridge `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` (iter-173 `chart-bridge173` recipe step (a));
17	* the per-chart ring maps `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` of the scaling action;
18	* the pullback cover `gmScalingP1_cover` of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`;

23	* the load-bearing fixed-point property `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`;

41	/-! ### Chart-bridge: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away _ _))`
42	
43	The helper below is the iter-173 `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe step (a)
44	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). Used by `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (below the `gmScalingP1_cover`
45	definition). -/
46	
47	/-- **`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 f))`** for any
48	homogeneous element `f` of positive degree.
49	
50	Generic in the element `f` AND in the degree `m` so we can apply it to either
51	`(![X 0, X 1]) i` (the actual chart input from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.f i`,
52	which uses `m = (![1, 1]) i`) or `X i` (which uses `m = 1`). A pure rewrite chasing
53	`awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` + the `algebraKbarAway` defeq. -/
54	private lemma awayι_comp_PLB_hom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
55	    {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
56	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar m) :
57	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f hf hm ≫
58	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
59	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
60	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f))) := by
61	  change Proj.awayι _ _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
62	  rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp,
63	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
64	  rfl
65	
66	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
67	
68	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
69	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
70	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
71	
72	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
73	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
74	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
75	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
76	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
77	
78	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
79	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
80	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
81	
82	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
83	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
84	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
85	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
86	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
87	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
88	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
89	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
90	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
91	
92	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
93	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
94	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
95	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
96	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
97	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
98	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
99	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
100	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
101	        GmRing kbar))
102	
103	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
104	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
105	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
106	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
108	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
109	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
110	
111	/-- **The chart-`i` source of `gmScalingP1_cover` is
112	`Spec ((Away 𝒜 ((![X 0, X 1]) i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.**
113	
114	Built by composing `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`, the
115	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite via `pullback.congrHom`, and `pullbackSpecIso`. Mirrors the

125	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ≅

441	of each factor). The `(X ⊗ Y).hom = pullback.fst ≫ X.hom` defeq unfolds, then
442	`GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` chains `pullback.fst`'s GI (by base-change stability of GI
443	from `gm_geomIrred`) with `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`.
444	
445	Exported here for Lane B (replaces its inline `haveI hProdGI`). Axiom-clean given the
446	individual GI scaffolds. -/
447	instance projGm_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
448	    GeometricallyIrreducible ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
449	  change GeometricallyIrreducible
450	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
451	  exact GeometricallyIrreducible.comp _ _
452	
453	/-- **`(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left` is reduced.** Project-side scaffold sorry (Mathlib gap: the
454	`Smooth → GeometricallyReduced` bridge is missing at scheme level, so the standard
455	`isReduced_of_flat_of_isLocallyNoetherian` route is not directly applicable without
456	adding scheme-level infrastructure beyond an iter-167 lane's scope).
457	
458	The chart-local alternative: cover `ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm` by `Spec(k̄[t, λ, λ⁻¹])`
459	(a domain over k̄) using the product of `Proj.affineOpenCover` and the affine
460	`Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`. Each chart is a domain ⟹ reduced. Both rely on bridges currently
461	absent in Mathlib (`HomogeneousLocalization.Away`-is-domain plus
462	`tensor-of-domains-over-field-is-domain`).
463	
464	Exported here for Lane B (replaces its inline `haveI hProdRed`). -/
465	instance projGm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
466	    IsReduced ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left := by
467	  -- Strategy: chart-local IsReduced via Proj.affineOpenCover product, each chart a domain.
468	  -- Currently sorry: blocked by Mathlib gap on Smooth → GeometricallyReduced.
469	  sorry
470	
471	end AlgebraicGeometry

13	What Mathlib idiom retires the two TEMPORARY axioms
14	`gmScalingP1_chart_data_temp` and `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero_temp` in
15	`AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean` (iter-177 HARD STOP
16	corrective)? Specifically: how to rewrite `cover.openCover.f i` back to
17	`Proj.awayι _ (X_i) _ _` form so the chart-bridge proof body chasing
18	`pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` succeeds.

24	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:175-194` — `gmScalingP1_chart` (axiom-clean def; uses the iso).

27	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean:175-221` — `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` via `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` (the cover whose `.f i` we need to identify with `Proj.awayι _ (X i) _ _`).
28	
29	## Decisions identified
30	
31	### Decision 1: Is `Scheme.AffineOpenCover.openCover_f` + `Matrix.cons_val` the right idiom?
32	
33	- **Mathlib idiom**: PARTIAL — `Scheme.AffineOpenCover.openCover_f`
34	  (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open`) gives `𝒰.openCover.f j = 𝒰.f j`
35	  by `rfl`. For `𝒰 = Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan 𝒜 f f_deg hm hf`,
36	  the field `.f i` is **definitionally** `Proj.awayι 𝒜 (f i) (f_deg i) (hm i)`
37	  (verified: generic `i`-form is `rfl` after a `let cov := …` introduces the
38	  cover and `f_deg`/`hm` are bound names, not inline `by` tactics).
39	- **Subtle structural blocker**: when the index is written `⟨0, _⟩`
40	  (NOT canonical `(0 : Fin 2)`), Lean's elaborator does NOT reduce
41	  `(![X 0, X 1]) ⟨0, _⟩` to `X 0`. Concretely verified: `f_deg ⟨0, by omega⟩ = hf₀`
42	  fails with a Type mismatch error, while `f_deg (0 : Fin 2) = hf₀` is `rfl`.
43	  The reason: `⟨0, h⟩` carries a *metavariable* proof at elaboration time;
44	  the elaborator never instantiates the `Fin.cons`/`Matrix.cons` computation
45	  rule unless the index normalizes to a canonical `Fin.OfNat` form.
46	- **Compounding factor**: when `f_deg`/`hm` are built inline via
47	  `(fun i => by fin_cases i <;> simp [...])`, the kernel must `whnf` the
48	  tactic-built proof terms in addition to the index reduction, which times
49	  out at 200k heartbeats (verified) and even at 800k (verified).
50	- **Project's current path**: `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` matches on `i`
51	  via `match i with | ⟨0, _⟩ => … | ⟨1, _⟩ => …`. In each branch, the body
52	  references `MvPolynomial.X 0` / `MvPolynomial.X 1` directly — but the
53	  target type uses `MvPolynomial.X i`. The match equation `i = ⟨0, _⟩`
54	  doesn't trigger the `Matrix.cons_val_zero` reduction needed to unify
55	  `(cover.openCover.f i = Proj.awayι 𝒜 ((![X 0, X 1]) ⟨0, _⟩) …)` with
56	  `(Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X 0) …)` inside `pullback.congrHom`.
57	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. Mathlib has the idiom (`openCover_f` is
58	  the right path), but the project's `match-on-i` decoration of
59	  `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` causes the iso to be **specialised** in each
60	  branch in a way that breaks the `Matrix.cons` defeq chain.
61	- **Cost of the current divergence**: 6 iters of helper-bridge attempts
62	  (iter-172 → iter-177) failed to penetrate. iter-177 HARD STOP corrective
63	  admitted two project axioms.
64	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — refactor `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`
65	  to be **uniform in `i`** (no `match`), so the bridge chain works
66	  generically and the `Matrix.cons_val` reduction happens INSIDE the cover's
67	  rfl-defeq, not at the `pullback.congrHom` boundary.
68	
69	### Decision 2: Does Mathlib ship a `Proj.openCover` / `Proj.coverByCharts` that bypasses the issue?
70	
71	- **Mathlib idiom**: PARTIAL.
72	  - `Proj.affineOpenCover` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic`):
73	    uses ALL homogeneous-degree-positive elements (not Fin-indexed); too coarse
74	    for the project's 2-chart setting.
75	  - `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` (already used by the project):
76	    Fin-indexed, `.f i = Proj.awayι 𝒜 (f i) (f_deg i) (hm i)` by `rfl`.
77	  - `Proj.openCoverOfMapIrrelevantEqTop` / `Proj.openCoverOfMapIrreleventEqTop`:
78	    different shape (covers `X` from a `RingHom A → Γ(X, ⊤)`), not applicable.
79	- **Project's current path**: uses `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan`,
80	  which IS the right Mathlib idiom — the structural issue is downstream
81	  (the `match` in `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`), not the cover choice itself.
82	- **Gap**: identical (project uses the canonical Mathlib idiom).
83	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** — no upstream refactor needed; the cover itself is
84	  Mathlib-aligned.

86	### Decision 3: Pivot estimate — uniform-in-`i` refactor of `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`
87	
88	- **Refactor signature**:
89	  ```lean
90	  private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
91	      (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ≅
92	        Spec (CommRingCat.of
93	          (TensorProduct kbar
94	            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
95	              ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
96	            (GmRing kbar))) :=
97	    pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
98	      pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
99	      pullback.congrHom
100	        (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar
101	          ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)
102	          (-- hoist this f_deg proof to a top-level `noncomputable def` in BareScheme.lean
103	           projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i))
104	        (show (Gm kbar).hom =
105	            Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
106	      pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)
107	  ```
108	  Key structural change: the iso target uses `((![X 0, X 1]) i)` instead of
109	  `(MvPolynomial.X i)`, eliminating the defeq pivot. Downstream, `chart_PLB_eq`
110	  does a fresh `fin_cases i` AFTER unfolding, with `Matrix.cons_val_zero`/`one`
111	  closing the chart-ring iso target — but now the bridge chain is uniform in
112	  `i`, so `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` applies cleanly.
113	- **Required companion definition** (in `BareScheme.lean`, ~5-8 LOC):
114	  hoist the `f_deg : ∀ i, (![X 0, X 1]) i ∈ 𝒜 1` proof from the inline `by`
115	  tactic in `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (currently `(fun i => by fin_cases i <;> simp [...])`)
116	  to a named `noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`. The
117	  `hm : ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1]) i` similarly hoisted to
118	  `projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm`. This makes the proof terms reduce
119	  cleanly without the kernel chasing tactic-built proof closures.
120	- **LOC delta estimate**:
121	  - Hoisted `f_deg`/`hm` defs in `BareScheme.lean`: ~12 LOC.
122	  - `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` body refactor (eliminate `match`): -20, +12 ⟹ net ~-8 LOC.
123	  - `gmScalingP1_chart` body: minor signature update for the iso target; ~+5 LOC.
124	  - `gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq` body retirement (was axiom, now proven): ~+35-50 LOC
125	    using the 10-step recipe from `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3.
126	  - `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement` body retirement: ~+30-50 LOC
127	    (Sub-task B from `chart-bridge-shared-helper.md`).
128	  - `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` body retirement: ~+15-25 LOC
129	    (chart-1 ring-map computation; the chart-1 ring map sends `u ↦ u ⊗ λ`,
130	    and `zeroPt` factors through chart-1 at `u = 0`, so the composite
131	    evaluates to `zeroPt` independently of `λ`).
132	- **Total**: ~85-130 LOC across 5 lanes (within iter-177's ~80-150 estimate).
133	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** with the uniform-in-`i` refactor.

135	### Decision 4: Alternative — valuative-criterion route to `ℙ¹ → A const` (bypass chart-bridge)
136	
137	- **Mathlib idiom**: PARTIAL.
138	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper.of_valuativeCriterion`
139	    (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ValuativeCriterion`): proves properness FROM
140	    the valuative criterion. We need the converse direction (use properness
141	    to extend a morphism), accessed via `ValuativeCriterion.existence`
142	    (extracts the existence half from `[IsProper]`).
143	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` / `Scheme.PartialMap`
144	    (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.RationalMap`): framework for partial
145	    morphisms with `domain : X.Opens` and `dense_domain`. Has the SHAPE
146	    needed.
147	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.valuativeCriterion_existence_aux`
148	    (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Proper`): proves the
149	    existence half OF the criterion FOR `Proj` (used in the project's
150	    `projectiveLineBar_isProper` derivation chain); not the converse.
151	- **What's MISSING in Mathlib**: there is no single shipped lemma of the
152	  form "if `f : U ⟶ Y` is a morphism from a dense open of a regular
153	  Noetherian scheme `X` to a proper scheme `Y`, and `X \ U` has codimension
154	  ≥ 1, then `f` extends uniquely to a morphism `X ⟶ Y`". This is
155	  exactly the content of Route A's `A.4.a Lemma 3.3 codim-1` lane
156	  (`AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, currently skeleton-only,
157	  Mathlib gap CRITICAL PATH per `STRATEGY.md`).
158	- **Project's current path**: chart-bridge route (option (c) Route C from
159	  iter-170). The valuative-criterion alternative was tracked as a
160	  pre-committed replacement candidate (STRATEGY.md Open Q) gated on
161	  iter-182 status review.
162	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong (the valuative-criterion alternative). The
163	  alternative requires building essentially the SAME infrastructure as
164	  Route A.4.a (which is the dominant positive-genus risk; substrate UNOWNED).
165	  For a genus-0 sub-case (extending `𝔸¹ → A` to `ℙ¹ → A`), the codim-1
166	  extension is a single DVR lift, not the full surface-divisor machinery
167	  Route A needs — but the project would still need:
168	    (i) Build `Scheme.PartialMap`-to-`Scheme.Hom` extension across a closed
169	        codim-1 reduced subscheme (here `{∞} ⊂ ℙ¹`),
170	    (ii) Apply `ValuativeCriterion.existence` (extracted from `[IsProper A]`)
171	         to the localised DVR `O_{ℙ¹, ∞}`, then
172	    (iii) Glue back to a morphism `ℙ¹ ⟶ A` (essentially
173	          `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` of the affine chart + a Spec-of-DVR
174	          patch at `∞`).
175	  Step (i) is genuinely new project material; step (iii) is the SAME
176	  `Cover.glueMorphisms` blocker that's biting the chart-bridge route.
177	- **Cost of the valuative alternative**: ~80-130 LOC for steps (i)-(iii)
178	  PLUS the same `glueMorphisms` infrastructure cost (which the chart-bridge
179	  refactor already pays for). NET: no savings, plus a fresh
180	  `RationalMap`-extension lemma that's not Mathlib-shipped.
181	- **Verdict**: **DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY** — stay on the chart-bridge route
182	  (Decisions 1-3 are the path). The valuative alternative was a sensible
183	  backup but does not eliminate the structural blocker; it just shifts it.

185	## Verdicts (summary)
186	
187	| Decision | Verdict | Severity |
188	|---|---|---|
189	| (1) `openCover_f` + `Matrix.cons_val` recipe, but `match`-on-i defeats it | ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB | critical |
190	| (2) `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` is the right cover; project already uses it | PROCEED | informational |
191	| (3) Uniform-in-`i` refactor of `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (~85-130 LOC) | PROCEED | informational |
192	| (4) Valuative-criterion alternative: no savings, shifts same blocker | DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY | informational |
193	
194	## Recommendation
195	
196	**Land the uniform-in-`i` refactor of `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`**
197	(Decisions 1 + 3). The two TEMPORARY axioms retire via a 3-step refactor:
198	
199	### Step 1 — Hoist tactic-built proof terms in `BareScheme.lean` (~12 LOC)
200	
201	Add two top-level named defs that replace the inline `by` tactics in
202	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover`:
203	
204	```lean
205	private noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg
206	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
207	    ∀ i, (![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1]) i ∈
208	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
209	  fun i => by
210	    fin_cases i <;> simp [Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
211	      MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X]
212	
213	private lemma projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm :
214	    ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i :=
215	  fun i => by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos
216	```
217	
218	Then refactor `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` to consume these by name
219	(instead of inline `by` tactics).
220	
221	### Step 2 — Refactor `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` uniform in `i` (~ -8 LOC net)
222	
223	Replace the `match i with | ⟨0, _⟩ => … | ⟨1, _⟩ => …` body with a
224	single uniform expression whose target type uses
225	`((![X 0, X 1]) i)` instead of `(MvPolynomial.X i)`. Pass the
226	hoisted `projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i` as the
227	homogeneity witness to `awayι_comp_PLB_hom`. The post-`congrHom`
228	pullback is now uniformly
229	`pullback (Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 (![X 0, X 1] i)))) (Gm.hom)`,
230	so `pullbackSpecIso kbar _ _` applies generically.
231	
232	### Step 3 — Retire the two temp axioms (~80-125 LOC across 3 bodies)
233	
234	1. **`gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq`** (currently axiom-laundered):
235	   follow the 10-step recipe in `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md`
236	   Decision 3 (steps 1-10 listed there with verbatim Mathlib citations).
237	   With the uniform iso from Step 2, `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` fires
238	   syntactically and the bridge chain closes generically.
239	
240	2. **`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`** (currently axiom-laundered, second
241	   conjunct): follow `chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Sub-task B —
242	   diagonal cases via `CategoryTheory.Limits.fst_eq_snd_of_mono_eq`
243	   (since `(cover).f i` is `IsOpenImmersion`, hence mono); cross cases
244	   `(0,1)/(1,0)` via the algebraic identity `λ·u = (1/t)·λ` in
245	   `Localization.Away t ⊗[kbar] GmRing` (per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q4).
246	
247	3. **`gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`** (currently axiom-laundered): once
248	   `gmScalingP1` is concrete, the chart-1 ring map sends `u ↦ u ⊗ λ` and
249	   `zeroPt` factors through chart-1 at `u = 0`, so the composite evaluates
250	   to `zeroPt` independently of `λ`. The proof chain: unfold
251	   `gmScalingP1`, apply `Cover.hom_ext` on the `cover` of
252	   `(ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm).left`, reduce on chart-1 to a ring-level identity in
253	   `MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing` (sending the constant `0`
254	   under `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`'s `X ()`-binding).
255	
256	### Reversal trigger
257	
258	If Step 2 cannot eliminate the `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` syntactic
259	mismatch even with the uniform-in-`i` refactor (e.g., the
260	`MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing` chart-ring-iso's kbar-algebra
261	preservation surfaces a fresh unification issue), iter-179 falls back
262	to the **partial `Fin.cases` refactor**: keep the `match` in
263	`gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` but use `Fin.cases` (which produces the
264	canonical `(0 : Fin 2)` index, not `⟨0, _⟩`) — see the experimental
265	finding below for verification that `(0 : Fin 2)` triggers the right
266	`Matrix.cons_val` reduction.
267	
268	## Experimental verification (iter-178)
269	
270	The diagnosis above was empirically verified via four `lean_run_code`
271	probes:
272	
273	1. **Generic `i`-form is rfl-defeq**: when `f_deg`/`hm` are named
274	   hypotheses (not inline `by` tactics) and the index is left generic,
275	   `cov.openCover.f i = Proj.awayι 𝒜 ((![f₀, f₁]) i) (f_deg i) (hm i)`
276	   closes by `rfl`. ✓
277	2. **Canonical `0 : Fin 2` form is rfl-defeq**: when `f_deg` is a named
278	   hypothesis and the index is `(0 : Fin 2)`,
279	   `cov.openCover.f (0 : Fin 2) = Proj.awayι 𝒜 f₀ hf₀ Nat.one_pos`
280	   closes by `rfl` (proof irrelevance handles the proof-term mismatch
281	   between `hf₀` and `f_deg 0`). ✓
282	3. **`⟨0, _⟩` form fails**: when the same goal uses `⟨0, by omega⟩` as
283	   the index, `rfl` times out at 800k heartbeats. The elaborator does
284	   NOT unfold `(![f₀, f₁]) ⟨0, _⟩` to `f₀`. ✗
285	4. **Inline `by`-tactic in cover construction compounds the failure**:
286	   when `f_deg`/`hm` are built inline via `(fun i => by fin_cases i <;> ...)`,
287	   even Step 2's `simp [openCover_f, Fin.mk_zero', Matrix.cons_val_zero]`
288	   followed by `rfl` times out. ✗
289	
290	The combination of (3) + (4) is precisely why the iter-176 analogist's
291	`simp [Fin.isValue, Fin.zero_eta]` recipe failed: the simp set normalized
292	the Fin indexing but didn't reach into the `f_deg`/`hm` tactic closures.
293

303	- The valuative-criterion alternative (Decision 4 DIVERGE: shifts blocker,

37	## Coordinate convention chosen for the rest of this file
38	
39	To avoid confusion between "chart i = D₊(X i)" and "chart i = where X i vanishes",
40	the rest of this document uses the unambiguous version:
41	
42	| Chart name | Open set | Vanishing coord | Affine coord | Where `zeroPt = [0:1]` lives |
43	|---|---|---|---|---|
44	| `chart₀` | `D₊(X 0)` | `X 1 = 0` allowed if `X 0 ≠ 0` | `t := X 1 / X 0` | NO (`X 0 = 0` at zeroPt) |
45	| `chart₁` | `D₊(X 1)` | `X 0 = 0` allowed if `X 1 ≠ 0` | `u := X 0 / X 1` | YES (`u = 0` at zeroPt) |
46	
47	Homogeneous scaling convention picked for `σ_×`:
48	`σ_×([X 0 : X 1], λ) = [λ·X 0 : X 1]` ⇒ `σ_×([0:1], λ) = [0:1] = zeroPt` ∀ λ (✓).
49	This means:
50	
51	- On `chart₁` (where the collapse-at-zero lemma lives): `u ↦ λ·u`, i.e. the ring
52	  map `MvPolynomial Unit k̄ → MvPolynomial Unit k̄ ⊗_{k̄} k̄[λ, λ⁻¹]`, `X () ↦ X () ⊗ λ`.
53	- On `chart₀`: `t = X 1/X 0 ↦ X 1/(λ·X 0) = t/λ`, i.e. `X () ↦ X () ⊗ λ⁻¹`.
54	
55	(The chart-0 map uses `λ⁻¹ ∈ GmRing`, available because GmRing is `Localization.Away (X ())`,
56	so `Localization.Away.invSelf` gives `λ⁻¹`. **This is essential** — the chart-0 map only
57	makes sense after tensor with Gm precisely because that's what makes `λ⁻¹` available.)
58

159	### Decision Q1: 2-chart cover of `ProjectiveLineBar`
160	
161	- **Mathlib idiom**: `affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` specialised to `ι := Fin 2`,
162	  `f := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]`, `m := ![1, 1]`. **EXACT** signature
163	  the prover writes:
164	
165	  ```lean
166	  /-- The 2-chart cover of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`'s underlying scheme by the two
167	  affine charts `D₊(X 0)` and `D₊(X 1)`. -/
168	  noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
169	      (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
170	    Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
171	      (ι := Fin 2)
172	      (f := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1])
173	      (m := ![1, 1])
174	      (f_deg := fun i ↦ by fin_cases i <;>
175	        exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar _)
176	      (hm := fun i ↦ by fin_cases i <;> exact one_pos)
177	      (hf := by
178	        -- The irrelevant ideal = (X 0, X 1), span of the two generators.
179	        intro p hp
180	        rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff] at hp
181	        -- `p` is in degree-positive part; decompose into X-monomials and conclude.
182	        sorry  -- standard MvPolynomial decomposition; ~10 LOC
183	        )
184	  ```
185	
186	  The `hf` step needs to show that the irrelevant ideal of the standard ℕ-grading on
187	  `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` is contained in `Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — a standard
188	  decomposition argument via `MvPolynomial.support_X`. **This is the only
189	  not-completely-trivial sub-step**; the rest is `fin_cases`.
190	
191	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED with `affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan`**.
192	- **Concrete checklist for the prover**: name the cover
193	  `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (NOT `projectiveLineBar...Cover`); use `Fin 2`
194	  as index (not `PNat × _` like the all-generators `Proj.affineOpenCover`).

197	### Decision Q2: `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`
198	
199	- **Mathlib idiom**: **NOT SHIPPED**. The previous iter-167 sketch said
200	  "~30 LOC, project owes". The deeper investigation at iter-168 reveals:
201	  `HomogeneousLocalization.Away` is *not* a localization-of-the-graded-ring (it's
202	  the *degree-0 sub-ring* of `Localization (Submonoid.powers f)` selected by the
203	  homogeneous-fraction structure). So the iso must be built from the `Away.mk`
204	  +  `val_injective` API directly.
205	
206	  **The canonical Mathlib path is the universal property of `MvPolynomial Unit`**:
207	
208	  ```lean
209	  /-- The chart-`i` ring `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≅ k̄[u]` where `u = X (1-i) / X i`. -/
210	  noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso
211	      (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
212	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
213	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
214	        MvPolynomial Unit kbar where
215	    -- Inverse direction (THE EASY HALF — universal property of MvPolynomial Unit):
216	    invFun := MvPolynomial.aeval (R := kbar)
217	      (S₁ := HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (X i))
218	      (fun _ => HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk
219	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
220	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) 1
221	        (MvPolynomial.X (jOf i))                  -- jOf i := if i = 0 then 1 else 0
222	        (by simp; exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (jOf i)))
223	    -- Forward direction (THE HARD HALF — defined by `Quotient.lift` via `val_injective`):
224	    toFun := Quotient.lift
225	      (fun ⟨n, ⟨num, hnum⟩, ⟨den, hden⟩, ⟨k, hk⟩⟩ =>
226	        -- evaluate the fraction `num / X i ^ k` at X i ↦ 1, X (jOf i) ↦ X ()
227	        MvPolynomial.eval (fun j => if j = i then (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
228	                                     else MvPolynomial.X ()) num)
229	      (by
230	        intro x y h
231	        -- The equivalence relation is `embedding x = embedding y`, both views in
232	        -- `Localization (powers (X i))`. Use the well-defined ring map
233	        -- `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄ → MvPolynomial Unit k̄`, `X i ↦ 1`, `X (jOf i) ↦ X ()`
234	        -- and the fact that `X i ↦ 1` makes powers of `X i` into 1 (units), so the
235	        -- map factors through Localization.
236	        sorry  -- ~10-15 LOC: invoke MvPolynomial.eval's well-definedness
237	        )
238	    map_one' := by simp [HomogeneousLocalization.one_eq, Quotient.lift]
239	    map_mul' := by
240	      rintro ⟨_⟩ ⟨_⟩
241	      simp [Quotient.lift]; ring  -- standard
242	    map_add' := by
243	      rintro ⟨_⟩ ⟨_⟩
244	      simp [Quotient.lift]; ring  -- standard
245	    map_zero' := by simp [HomogeneousLocalization.zero_eq, Quotient.lift]
246	    left_inv := by
247	      rintro ⟨n, ⟨num, hnum⟩, ⟨den, hden⟩, ⟨k, rfl⟩⟩
248	      -- Round-trip: forward sends to `eval`; backward sends back to `Away.mk`.
249	      -- Use `HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective` to compare in `Localization`.
250	      apply HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective
251	      sorry  -- ~15 LOC: chase via `Away.val_mk` + `Localization.mk_eq_mk_iff`
252	    right_inv := by
253	      intro p
254	      -- Round-trip: backward sends to `Away.mk` of `aeval`; forward evaluates.
255	      induction p using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
256	      | C r => simp [MvPolynomial.aeval_C]; rfl
257	      | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
258	      | mul_X p _ hp => simp [hp]; ring
259	  ```
260	
261	  where `jOf : Fin 2 → Fin 2` is the "swap" `if i = 0 then 1 else 0`.
262	
263	- **Mathlib gap depth**: this is NOT shipped at all. The iter-167 file's "~30 LOC"
264	  estimate was too optimistic — the realistic count is **60-90 LOC** because:
265	  - The "forward" map's well-definedness goes through `Quotient.lift`'s
266	    well-definedness hypothesis on the embedding into `Localization`, which is
267	    where the actual ring-theoretic content lives.
268	  - `MvPolynomial.eval` is over a polynomial-ring substitution that the `Quotient.lift`
269	    needs to commute with — this is **not a single Mathlib lemma**.
270	  - The `left_inv` round-trip needs `HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective` plus
271	    a direct comparison in `Localization`.
272	
273	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL**. The project owes this helper. It is
274	  upstream-able as a single ~80-LOC file `Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization.PolynomialQuotient`
275	  (or similar). Out of scope for landing in Mathlib at iter-168; landing in the
276	  project is required for `gmScalingP1`.
277	
278	  **PROCEED with the recipe above**, but treat the LOC estimate as ~60-90, not
279	  ~30. The previous file's optimistic estimate is what blocked iter-167 — the
280	  prover ran out of budget on a "30 LOC" item that was 3x larger.

282	- **Alternative considered and rejected**: use `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top`
283	  (`Mathlib/RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/HomogeneousLocalization.lean:1064`) +
284	  `Algebra.adjoin_eq_top_iff_isIso_aeval`. **Rejected**: this still requires

288	### Decision Q3: chart-side morphism via `pullbackSpecIso`
289	
290	- **Setup**:
291	  - Chart `i` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left` = `Spec (Away 𝒜 (X i))`. After Q2's
292	    iso, identify with `Spec (MvPolynomial Unit k̄)`.
293	  - `Gm.left = Spec GmRing = Spec (Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄))`.
294	  - Product `chart_i ⊗ Gm` in `Over (Spec k̄)` = pullback of the two structure
295	    maps to `Spec k̄`. Via `pullbackSpecIso`, this is
296	    `Spec ((MvPolynomial Unit k̄) ⊗_{k̄} GmRing)`.
297	
298	- **The chart-1 morphism (the one needed by collapse-at-zero)**:
299	  Ring map at chart 1:
300	  ```
301	  φ₁ : MvPolynomial Unit k̄  →+*  MvPolynomial Unit k̄ ⊗_{k̄} GmRing
302	  φ₁(X ()) = X () ⊗ₜ algebraMap kbar GmRing 1 * 1 ⊗ₜ X ()_Gm   -- = u·λ
303	  ```
304	  built concretely via `MvPolynomial.aeval`:
305	  ```lean
306	  noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
307	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar :=
308	    (MvPolynomial.aeval
309	      (R := kbar) (σ := Unit)
310	      (S₁ := MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)
311	      (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ
312	                  (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar) 1) *
313	                (1 : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ
314	                  (algebraMap _ (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))).toRingHom
315	  ```
316	
317	  **WARNING**: this is hard to read but the simple way to spell it is "u ↦ u·λ
318	  in the tensor product", which by `Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul` equals
319	  `(u ⊗ 1) * (1 ⊗ λ) = u ⊗ λ`. So a cleaner spelling:
320	
321	  ```lean
322	  noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap :
323	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar :=
324	    MvPolynomial.eval₂RingHom
325	      (algebraMap kbar _)
326	      (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ
327	                  (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)
328	                    (MvPolynomial.X ())))
329	  ```
330	
331	  (Uses `MvPolynomial.eval₂RingHom` to send `X () ↦ u ⊗ λ` and `kbar ↦ kbar ⊗ 1`.)
332	
333	- **The chart-side morphism in `Scheme`**:
334	  ```lean
335	  -- chart 1's map "(u, λ) ↦ λ·u" — into ProjectiveLineBarScheme directly:
336	  noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
337	      pullback
338	          (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))))
339	          (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))) ⟶
340	        ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar :=
341	    -- 1. Bridge: pullback ≅ Spec (MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)
342	    (pullbackSpecIso kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)).hom ≫
343	    -- 2. The ring map: u ↦ u ⊗ λ, defined in `gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap`
344	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap kbar)) ≫
345	    -- 3. The reverse iso k[u] ≃+* Away 𝒜 (X 1)
346	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).symm) ≫
347	    -- 4. The awayι into Proj
348	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (X 1)
349	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1) one_pos
350	  ```
351	
352	  **WARNING (subtle)**: the "chart of `ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm`" is NOT literally
353	  `pullback (Spec.map _) (Spec.map _)`; it's the `Over (Spec k̄)`-level product
354	  `chart_i ⊗ Gm`, whose underlying `Scheme.pullback` is `pullback awayι.hom Gm.hom`
355	  (NOT `pullback (algebraMap _ _) (algebraMap _ _)`). To bridge, the prover must
356	  use the explicit description of `(chart_i ⊗ Gm).left`:
357	
358	  ```lean
359	  (Spec (.of (Away 𝒜 (X i))) ⊗ Gm kbar).left =
360	    pullback (chart structure map) (Gm structure map)
361	  ```
362	
363	  Both structure maps factor through `Spec k̄`, so by uniqueness of pullback this
364	  IS isomorphic to `pullback (Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 (X i))))
365	  (Spec.map (algebraMap kbar GmRing))`, which is what `pullbackSpecIso` expects.
366	  Practically, the prover writes the chart map at the `Over (Spec k̄)` level via
367	  `Over.homMk` and lets the `Over`-pullback API do the bridging.
368	
369	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED with `pullbackSpecIso` + ring map + reverse Q2-iso + `awayι`**.
370	  The ring map's definition is short (~5 LOC); the wrapping through `Over` /
371	  `pullbackSpecIso` is `~15 LOC`. **Critical**: the prover MUST work in
372	  `Over (Spec k̄)` from the start, or the `(chart ⊗ Gm)`-vs-`pullback awayι Gm.hom`
373	  mismatch will require manual bridging at every `simp`.
374

379	  -- `Localization.Away`-invertible-element witness; `Mathlib/RingTheory/Localization/Away/Basic.lean`).

383	      (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ

390	### Decision Q4: cross-chart agreement equation
391	
392	- **What `glueMorphisms` requires** (per `Gluing.lean:436`):
393	  ```
394	  ∀ x y : 𝒰.I₀, pullback.fst (𝒰.f x) (𝒰.f y) ≫ f x = pullback.snd _ _ ≫ f y
395	  ```
396	  For our 2-element cover `𝒰 = projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.pullback₁ pullback.fst`,
397	  the `x = y` cases are trivial (`pullback.fst = pullback.snd` on the diagonal,
398	  up to `pullback.condition`). The `(0, 1)` and `(1, 0)` cases are the real
399	  agreement check.
400	
401	- **The (0, 1)-case**: on the intersection `D₊(X 0) ∩ D₊(X 1) = D₊(X 0 · X 1)`,
402	  affine ring `Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1)`. Via Mathlib's `Proj.Away.isLocalization_mul`
403	  (`RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/HomogeneousLocalization.lean:883`), this is
404	  `Localization.Away (isLocalizationElem (X 0)_deg (X 1)_deg)` on top of
405	  `Away 𝒜 (X 0)`, which after Q2's chart-0 iso is
406	  `Localization.Away (X ())` on top of `MvPolynomial Unit k̄`, i.e.
407	  `Localization.Away t = k̄[t, t⁻¹]`.
408	
409	  After tensoring with `GmRing = k̄[λ, λ⁻¹]`, the intersection ring is
410	  `k̄[t, t⁻¹] ⊗_{k̄} k̄[λ, λ⁻¹] = k̄[t, t⁻¹, λ, λ⁻¹]`.
411	
412	  **The ring-level equation**: both chart maps, restricted to the intersection,
413	  send `u = X 0 / X 1 ∈ Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1)` (where `u = 1/t` on chart-0 side,
414	  `u = u` on chart-1 side) to the same expression `t ↦ λ·u ↔ λ/t` on chart-0
415	  and to `u ↦ λ·u` on chart-1. They're equal because `λ·u = λ/t = λ·(1/t)`.
416	
417	  **Practical form** (what the prover types):
418	
419	  ```lean
420	  -- For x = 0, y = 1 (the cross case):
421	  · -- Goal: pullback.fst _ _ ≫ chart0_map = pullback.snd _ _ ≫ chart1_map
422	    -- on Spec(MvPolynomial Unit k̄ ⊗ MvPolynomial Unit k̄ ⊗ GmRing) (~)
423	    -- The two ring maps coincide on the (1-1)/(1-0) coordinate after multiplying by t
424	    -- (= passing through Localization.Away t). Use:
425	    apply (pullbackSpecIso _ _ _).hom.eq_of_comp_left  -- or similar
426	    -- then unfold and apply `MvPolynomial.algHom_ext` + `t * (1/t) = 1`.
427	    sorry
428	  ```
429	
430	  **The actual ring-level identity**: in `MvPolynomial Unit k̄ ⊗
431	  MvPolynomial Unit k̄ ⊗ GmRing`, when restricted to the chart-intersection
432	  localization, the chart-0 expression `(1/t) ⊗ λ⁻¹` and the chart-1 expression
433	  `u ⊗ λ` get identified via the gluing iso `t = 1/u, u = 1/t`. Concretely, on
434	  the intersection ring `Localization.Away t ⊗ GmRing`, the chart-0 map sends
435	  `1/t ↦ (1/t)·λ⁻¹` and the chart-1 map (after `t = 1/u` substitution) sends
436	  `u ↦ u·λ = (1/t)·λ`. **They differ by a factor of `λ²`**. Wait — that's wrong.
437	  Let me recompute.
438	
439	  Actually: chart-0 affine coord t = X 1/X 0, chart-1 affine coord u = X 0/X 1.
440	  So on the intersection, `t·u = 1`, i.e. `u = 1/t`. Under σ_×:
441	  - Chart-0: `t ↦ t/λ` (NOT `t ↦ λ·t`). After convention chosen above
442	    (`σ_× = [λX 0 : X 1]`): `t = X 1/X 0 ↦ X 1/(λ X 0) = t/λ`.
443	  - Chart-1: `u ↦ λ·u`.
444	  - On intersection: `λ·u = λ·(1/t) = λ/t`, vs `t ↦ t/λ` means `u = 1/t ↦ 1/(t/λ) = λ/t`. **EQUAL.** ✓
445	
446	  So the cross-chart agreement reduces to `λ·u = 1/(t/λ)`, which after
447	  `u = 1/t` and `Localization.Away t`'s invertibility is `λ·(1/t)·t = λ`, true.
448	
449	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED with the explicit ring-level identity** `λ·u = (1/t)·λ`
450	  inside `Localization.Away t ⊗ GmRing`. The proof script is:
451	  ```lean
452	  · -- Cross-chart agreement at (i, j) = (0, 1)
453	    -- Goal: pullback.fst _ _ ≫ chart0_map = pullback.snd _ _ ≫ chart1_map
454	    -- Reduce both sides to a Spec.map of the SAME ring map on
455	    -- Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1)) ⊗_{k̄} GmRing).
456	    -- This is a `pullbackSpecIso` chain + a `MvPolynomial.algHom_ext` /
457	    -- IsLocalization.lift_uniq closure.
458	    ext1
459	    · -- on Spec coord (1/t)
460	      simp [gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap, gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap,

1	\chapter{Abelian-variety rigidity: morphisms from a genus-$0$ curve into an abelian variety are constant}
2	\label{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/ChartIso.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean AlgebraicJacobian/RigidityLemma.lean
4	
5	% Local macro: diagrammatic (Lean `≫`) composition. Defined locally because this
6	% chapter is the writer's only editable file; flagged in the writer report for promotion
7	% to macros/common.tex if desired. \providecommand is a no-op if it is later added globally.
8	\providecommand{\fatsemi}{\mathbin{\,;\,}}
9	
10	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-157). This chapter is the dedicated home of the project's COMMITTED
11	% genus-0 route (route (c)): the characteristic-free abelian-variety rigidity stack. It is
12	% 1:1 with the new upstream Lean file AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean, which
13	% imports AlgebraicJacobian.Genus and is imported by AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian (breaking the
14	% RigidityKbar -> Rigidity -> Jacobian import cycle so that genusZeroWitness can consume the
15	% keystone directly). The headline thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV is the char-free
16	% replacement (no [CharZero]) for the over-k-bar declaration rigidity_over_kbar of
17	% \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (which remains in tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and
18	% still carries [CharZero]). The df=0 / H^0(P^1,Omega)=0 / Serre-duality framing is the
19	% fallback (a), NOT the route developed here.
20	
21	The genus-$0$ arm of the Jacobian-witness construction has a \emph{trivial} witness object
22	($J = \Spec k$); its only remaining mathematical content is a rigidity \emph{statement}:
23	every pointed morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus $0$
24	into an abelian variety is the constant morphism at the identity. We prove this
25	\textbf{characteristic-free} --- the protected goal is over an arbitrary field $k$, so the
26	cheaper differential argument (via $H^0(\mathbb P^1, \Omega) = H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal
27	O(-2)) = 0$, hence $df = 0$, hence $f$ constant) is unavailable: it breaks on a
28	characteristic-$p$ Frobenius-descent wall, where a nonconstant $f$ can have $df = 0$. The
29	rigidity route below sidesteps that wall.
30	
31	The chain has two parts. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford,
32	Form~I; equivalently Milne's Rigidity Theorem~1.1) is an elementary properness/closed-map
33	argument --- it uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, is the entry point
34	for formalisation, and is now proven axiom-clean. From the Rigidity Lemma, Milne's \S I.3
35	derives the genus-$0$ base case \emph{without} the theorem of the cube --- and, in this
36	project's reading, \emph{without} Milne's Theorem~3.2 (rational-map extension), Lemma~3.3,
37	Auslander--Buchsbaum, or differentials (the ``4th route''; see
38	\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}). The decisive observation is that $\mathbb P^1$ is
39	\emph{already complete} and $f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A$ is \emph{already a total morphism}, so
40	there is no rational defect map to extend over a complete surface and Theorem~3.2 never enters.
41	Instead the base case is closed by the \textbf{$\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut} (the project's
42	primary route, an Archon-original realisation of Milne Proposition~3.9): one uses the
43	\emph{multiplicative scaling} action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to
44	\mathbb P^1$, $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x$ (the M\"obius scaling fixing both $0$ and
45	$\infty$), which is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb
46	G_m$ (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}). Normalising $f(0) = \eta_A$ and composing $h := \sigma_\times
47	\fatsemi f$, one applies additivity of $\mathrm{Hom}(-, A)$ over a product
48	(\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}, Milne Corollary~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires
49	only the \emph{first} factor complete --- with $V = \mathbb P^1$ proper, $W = \mathbb G_m$, and
50	base points $0 \in \mathbb P^1$, $1 \in \mathbb G_m$. Because $0$ is a \emph{fixed point} of
51	scaling, the $W$-axis restriction $\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0)$ is
52	\emph{constant} at $\eta_A$, so the decomposition collapses to $h = p \fatsemi f$, i.e.\
53	$f(\lambda x) = f(x)$. Specialising $x = 1$ gives that $f|_{\mathbb G_m}$ is constant, and since
54	$\mathbb G_m$ is dense in $\mathbb P^1$ with $A$ separated, $f$ is constant
55	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, via the separated-target/dominant-source handle
56	\cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}). This route uses \emph{only} the proven Corollary~1.5, a
57	scaling fixed point, and density --- it never forms the additive defect map, never invokes
58	$\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$, and so sidesteps the ``image of a group homomorphism is a
59	closed subgroup'' input that has no Mathlib support. The classical $\mathbb G_a$-additive
60	argument (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} via the $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility
61	\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, the heart of Milne Proposition~3.9) is retained only as the
62	Milne-faithful alternative and is \emph{off} the genus-$0$ critical path. The corollary that a
63	pointed regular map of abelian
64	varieties is a homomorphism (\cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom}, Milne Corollary~1.2) is recorded
65	for completeness and for Route~A but is \emph{not} needed on the genus-$0$ path; Milne's
66	Theorem~3.2 (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is likewise demoted to a Route-A-only item
67	(the Albanese universal property), no longer on the genus-$0$ critical path
68	(\cref{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}). Combining these
69	gives that every morphism $\mathbb P^1 \to A$ is constant
70	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); composing with the classification of genus-$0$
71	curves (\cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}, $C \cong \mathbb P^1$ over $\bar k$) yields the
72	headline \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. The theorem of the cube (Milne \S I.5) is
73	\emph{not} used anywhere on this path (see \cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}); the chain invokes
74	neither Serre duality nor the FGA $\Pic$ representability engine, so the genus-$0$ arm is
75	fully decoupled from Route~A.
76	
77	\medskip
78	
79	Throughout, $\bar k$ is an algebraically closed field of arbitrary characteristic. An
80	\emph{abelian variety} over $\bar k$ is a complete (proper) irreducible group variety; in the
81	project's Lean encoding $A$ is an object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$ carrying
82	$\mathtt{GrpObj}$, $\mathtt{IsProper}$, $\mathtt{Smooth}$, and $\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}$.
83

1408	  $\mathrm{Proj.away}\,\iota$ to land in $\mathbb P^1.\mathrm{left}$. The substantive step that is
